review_gates.notes: Splitting the core-ops profile into a standalone repo; generic-agent adapter is the portable surface and runtime-specific adapters are re-generated downstream.
core-ops kernel, profile-index entry, and skill files are copied as authoritative sources; generated manifests remain derived artifacts in the destination repo.
profile-first entrypoint and full-root delegation semantics are carried over via selection_semantics in the manifest and the kernel adapters block.
Source inventory, file copy list, and exclusion list reviewed manually before scaffolding the destination repo.
Pre-split: acceptance_checks in source repo. Post-split: re-run exporter and validator against copied kernel in destination repo plus compatibility test that source repo still passes after removal.
Audited core-ops kernel, profile-index, and skill bodies for credentials, raw logs, private memory paths, and machine-specific user paths; only public agent-neutral metadata present.
Grepped destination file copy list for token, secret, ~/Projects, .agent/sidecars, and raw debug log patterns; none found.

privacy_audit.notes: Verified that no skill body, kernel field, or profile-index entry references private memory paths, credentials, or local machine user paths before the public destination repo is scaffolded.

rollback.restore_strategy: Record destination repo URL, commit hash before scaffolding, and exact file copy list; on rollback, delete the destination repo (or revert its first commit) and restore the source profile-index entry from the rollback record.
